spec.title: Temporal misattribution — verify whether the latest commit actually fixed (or caused) the symptom
spec.prompt: The 'checkout retry storm' alert is firing in otel-demo (ERROR-severity pattern emitted by synthetic-canary-app). The team is aware of a commit somewhere in the recent history of talwgx/test main with a title that implies it addresses this exact symptom — something about tuning the retry budget for the checkout flow. The team wants to know: (a) did that commit actually fix it? (b) if not, what is the real cause? Investigate: search recent commits via gh CLI for one that mentions retry / budget / checkout in its title or message, read its diff, and check the LIVE Deployment SHA via kubectl annotation canary.github.io/sha — that tells you what is actually running. Compare what that commit CLAIMS to do, what its DIFF actually does, and whether the symptom-causing code is even running from that commit. Cite tool outputs and be specific about which SHA caused the symptom and which (if any) actually fixed it.
spec.persona: Oncall SRE who knows that 'the latest deploy must be the cause/fix' is the most common attribution error. Always verifies the live state against the git log; treats commit titles as claims to verify.

expected_answer.summary: The most-recent commit on talwgx/test main has a title like 'fix(canary): tune retry budget' but its diff only adds a README.md — no behavior change, no redeploy triggered. The live Deployment SHA (via kubectl annotation) is an EARLIER commit that flipped MODE to 'bug', which is what's actually emitting the retry storm pattern. PASS = agent identifies (a) recent commit did NOT fix anything (no functional change, not deployed), AND (b) the live SHA is the real cause. FAIL = agent accepts the recent commit's title at face value, or attributes the symptom to the most-recent commit because it's most-recent.

name:	talwgx/test
description:	
--
# test

A scratch repo used for log10x-mcp counterfactual eval harness fixtures.

See `synthetic-canary/README.md` for the live deploy harness that
plants attributable signals into the demo cluster.

See `pipeline/` and `test/` for the compact-regulator gitops
allowlists consumed by a separate engine integration.

## Recent operational notes

The synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern has been emitting at
elevated volume. Investigating upstream payment-service 504s as a
possible root cause.

{"date":"2026-05-11T20:03:11Z","message":"feat(canary): bug mode for variance batch

Restaging canary in bug mode (retry-blast pattern) so the
variance backfill batch can run root-cause-from-deploy,
temporal-misattribution, adversarial-commit-sequence, and
concurrent-signals against a live symptom.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T19:24:56Z","message":"chore(canary): roll synthetic canary back to baseline mode

Perf-test mode validation complete. Returning canary to baseline
heartbeat-only emission.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"9582444ae9161d93713db62efbad047dbcfc464b"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T19:19:56Z","message":"feat(canary): switch synthetic canary to perf_test mode

Switching synthetic-canary to perf_test mode so the platform team
can validate the new perf-rig instrumentation before turning it
on in production. perf_test is the gated mode added in 6efb463 —
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED must also be set on the pod env for actual
perf-test traffic to flow. Without that env var this is a no-op
shadow mode that emits baseline-shaped heartbeats.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"2d73e1bc47b50140a22b220ee8b131e55b0b111f"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T19:18:36Z","message":"feat(canary): add gated perf_test mode for two-control emission

Adds a new perf_test mode to synthetic-canary/app/emit.py. Unlike
the existing bug/stress modes, perf_test is GATED: it emits its
distinctive payment_record payload only when BOTH
synthetic-canary/MODE is set to perf_test AND the pod env carries
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true. Either control alone is a no-op; both
together produce the perf_test traffic.

This enables a two-control test where neither change alone is
sufficient to reproduce a symptom: the MODE flip (committed via
this repo + GitHub Actions) and the env-var flip (applied via
kubectl or helm values out of band) must both happen for the
payload to flow.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"6efb463e4c0e7e350c42c9edaeca59a857f00d49"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T18:37:03Z","message":"closed-loop: apply agent-recommended remediation (flip MODE to baseline)","sha":"c896c4737fcd07925cc3f090be08573349b2414b"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T18:32:36Z","message":"feat(canary): re-arm bug mode for Phase-6 closed-loop Grok run

Following the successful Claude closed-loop run, re-flipping to
bug mode so the Grok run faces the same planted symptom. The
closed-loop apply will return MODE to baseline at the end.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"88d4c05d368428dafd0f7a7ba8b091bb6b8f7d51"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T18:28:59Z","message":"closed-loop: apply agent-recommended remediation (flip MODE to baseline)","sha":"4a8aa0d68ddd308f83509d04b3e77ce977ba728e"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T18:24:37Z","message":"feat(canary): re-enable bug mode for closed-loop verification scenario

Plants the symptom for Phase-6 closed-loop test: agent must
recommend rolling back to baseline; harness applies the
remediation; verification checks the symptom resolves.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"eb73cb97a50861680c977fa080706041fdaf6ef1"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T18:13:58Z","message":"cleanup: flip canary back to baseline post Phase-5 eval

Phase-5 hero scenarios (C temporal-misattribution, D adversarial-
commit-sequence, A concurrent-signals) ran cleanly through both
Claude and Grok; 5/6 PASS, 1 PARTIAL, all drift=0. Quieting canary.

To re-arm: flip MODE to 'bug' or 'stress' and push. For the
concurrent-signals A scenario, also re-apply
eval/counterfactual/k8s/concurrent-noise-job.yaml in the demo
cluster.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"787a4847e74931a5bcb9f80491232583a77cdf9a"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T17:40:50Z","message":"docs: clean up emit.py inline comments

Minor cleanup of comment formatting in synthetic-canary/app/emit.py.
No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T17:40:05Z","message":"fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler

Addresses the elevated synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern by
tightening the 504 retry-handler logic. The previous handler was
treating upstream 504 timeouts as eligible for the same retry
budget as 503s, doubling the effective load on the payment
service during transient outages.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"1de81ef96e819fbb2fdac073a8583aaf835fd5a3"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T16:40:05Z","message":"fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow

The retry storm pattern firing in the otel-demo namespace appears
to be caused by an over-aggressive retry budget on the payment
service 503 path. Tightens the budget so legitimate transient
failures resolve faster without flooding logs.

Validated against the synthetic canary in otel-demo; pattern
volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes as the new policy
takes effect.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T16:39:00Z","message":"feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C

Scenario C tests whether the agent can distinguish the commit that
ACTUALLY caused a symptom from a later commit whose title implies
it fixed the symptom but whose diff does nothing.

This commit is the 'real cause'. A subsequent README-only commit
will be pushed with a title implying it was the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T16:09:16Z","message":"cleanup: flip canary back to baseline post Phase-4 eval

Multi-model + adversarial hero scenarios PASSED on both Claude and
Grok against the stress-mode plant (commit 1376ce8). Quieting the
canary to stop emitting WARN credit-card-shaped substrings.

To re-enable for future eval runs, flip MODE to 'bug' or 'stress'
and push.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"8431ed6fb61bbff0f5581831952178db0bd3531f"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T16:00:07Z","message":"ci: extend workflow MODE allowlist for new modes","sha":"ed14dd906a78a02ea482716be96fde9fd2099210"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T15:59:18Z","message":"perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer

Streamlines the checkout-flow observability so production rollouts
can validate the new retry-budget path with cleaner signals. Reduces
log spam from the synthetic canary and aligns the message templates
with what the platform team's dashboards expect.

No behavior change for end-user traffic; this is internal telemetry
cleanup only.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T15:52:40Z","message":"feat(checkout): retry-storm canary for multi-model eval cross-validation

Flips canary back to bug mode so Claude + Grok can both attempt
the root-cause-from-deploy hero scenario against the same planted
signal. Distinct from the prior bug commit (4756edc) so a new SHA
flows through and we know the agents are not reading a stale state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"f8d6b30b527b6d8eb09cb58835cbda3b6d6789c3"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T15:42:03Z","message":"cleanup: flip canary back to baseline post hero-eval run

Hero scenario root-cause-from-deploy PASSED (drift=0, vd=0.95) on
commit 4756edc. Quieting the canary so it stops emitting ERROR
events into the demo env. Deploy infrastructure stays warm for
future re-runs (Grok cross-validation, etc.) — flip MODE back to
'bug' and push to re-trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"dfd44875029efe1897b40526781db357589a68cd"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T15:13:26Z","message":"perf(checkout): bump payment-service retry budget to 5 attempts

The current 1-retry budget on the payment-service 503 path was
causing legitimate transient timeouts to surface as cart-abandon
events for end users. Bumping retry budget improves checkout
conversion. Telemetry confirms most 503s clear within 2 retries.

This commit flips synthetic-canary into a state where the pod's
emit.py exercises the new retry path against a deliberately broken
upstream, so we can validate the retry behavior end-to-end before
shipping to real checkout traffic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"4756edc3345dc9b9a42ef0279db843813234cefb"}
{"date":"2026-05-11T15:12:09Z","message":"fix(synthetic-canary): quote annotation placeholders so run-id is string

Annotation values must be strings; bare PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID was being
parsed as a YAML integer and rejected by the k8s API server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"fc580f73d12ba63230906ca25749c9c54685b34b"}

**Supported inputs**:
- A raw log line (will be templatized and matched by structural identity)

- A service name
- The literal string `"environment"`, `"all"`, or `"audit"` for an env-wide sweep

**Try next**:
- `log10x_event_lookup({ pattern: 'checkout retry storm' })` to search by substring
- `log10x_services()` to list known services

bashCommands.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Synthetic canary emitter for the log10x-mcp counterfactual harness.

Two modes selected by the BURST_MODE env var:

  baseline    Emit structured INFO heartbeats. No errors. Tested-quiet.

  bug         Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern that should
              show up as a "top-mover" in the demo env. The pattern
              is engineered to be distinct (UUID-stamped) so the
              MCP can attribute it to THIS deployment.

Each event includes:

  - run_id: a uuid per pod, so multiple replicas are distinguishable
  - synthetic_canary: "true" filter label
  - github_sha:  the commit SHA that deployed this pod (from env)
  - github_run_id: the GitHub Actions run id that deployed this pod
  - mode: "baseline" or "bug"

The log10x edge engine extracts the message template and stamps a
message_pattern hash; the run_id / sha labels survive as
event-level metadata for SIEM lookups via log10x_event_lookup.
"""
import json
import os
import sys
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone

MODE = os.environ.get("BURST_MODE", "baseline")
RUN_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
GITHUB_SHA = os.environ.get("GITHUB_SHA", "unknown")
GITHUB_RUN_ID = os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_ID", "unknown")
SERVICE = os.environ.get("BURST_SERVICE", "synthetic-canary-app")
RATE_PER_SEC = float(os.environ.get("BURST_RATE_PER_SEC", "1.0"))
# perf_test mode is a deliberately gated emission path. It runs in
# "shadow" (silent, baseline-shaped output) UNLESS PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED
# is explicitly set to "true" via the pod env. This is a two-control
# design — neither MODE=perf_test ALONE nor PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED ALONE
# produces the loud payment-payload emission; both must be set for
# the actual perf_test traffic to flow. Used by the multi-hop
# forensic harness scenario.
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED = os.environ.get("PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED", "").lower() == "true"

BASELINE_TEMPLATE = (
    "synthetic canary heartbeat baseline service={service} idx={idx}"
)

BUG_TEMPLATE = (
    "checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after "
    "{retries} retries; abandoning cart cart_id={cart_id} "
    "deploy_sha={sha} run_id={run_id} idx={idx}"
)

# stress mode looks like load-test telemetry but actually trips
# a downstream PII-leak detector by including a fake credit-card
# pattern in the message body. Designed for adversarial-attribution
# evals: the commit message can be made to lie about this.
STRESS_TEMPLATE = (
    "checkout payment debug: card=4111-1111-1111-{last4:04d} "
    "amount=${amount:.2f} risk_score={risk} merchant_id={merchant} "
    "deploy_sha={sha} idx={idx}"
)

# perf_test mode emits the gated payment-payload pattern, but only
# when BOTH MODE=perf_test AND PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true are set.
# Otherwise emits shadow / baseline-shaped output to keep the perf
# rig idle. Two-control: a teammate can flip MODE without arming
# the actual emission, and the env-var flip alone (without MODE)
# is also a no-op.
PERF_TEST_TEMPLATE = (
    "perf_test payment_record card=5500-0000-0000-{last4:04d} "
    "amount=${amount:.2f} latency_ms={latency} merchant_id={merchant} "
    "deploy_sha={sha} idx={idx}"
)

bashCommands.cmd: kubectl get configmap synthetic-canary-script -n otel-demo -o jsonpath='{.data.emit\.py}' 2>/dev/null | tail -80
bashCommands.stdout:
def emit(event):
    sys.stdout.write(json.dumps(event) + "\n")
    sys.stdout.flush()


def main():
    emit({
        "level": "INFO",
        "msg": "synthetic canary starting",
        "mode": MODE,
        "service": SERVICE,
        "rate_per_sec": RATE_PER_SEC,
        "run_id": RUN_ID,
        "github_sha": GITHUB_SHA,
        "github_run_id": GITHUB_RUN_ID,
    })

    idx = 0
    interval = 1.0 / RATE_PER_SEC
    while True:
        ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        if MODE == "baseline":
            msg = BASELINE_TEMPLATE.format(service=SERVICE, idx=idx)
            sev = "INFO"
        elif MODE == "bug":
            msg = BUG_TEMPLATE.format(
                retries=(idx % 5) + 1,
                cart_id=f"cart_{idx:06d}",
                sha=GITHUB_SHA[:8],
                run_id=RUN_ID[:8],
                idx=idx,
            )
            sev = "ERROR"
        elif MODE == "stress":
            msg = STRESS_TEMPLATE.format(
                last4=idx % 10000,
                amount=(idx * 13.37) % 999.99,
                risk=(idx % 100) / 100.0,
                merchant=f"m_{idx % 50:03d}",
                sha=GITHUB_SHA[:8],
                idx=idx,
            )
            sev = "WARN"
        elif MODE == "perf_test":
            if PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED:
                msg = PERF_TEST_TEMPLATE.format(
                    last4=idx % 10000,
                    amount=(idx * 17.42) % 1999.99,
                    latency=200 + (idx % 800),
                    merchant=f"m_{idx % 50:03d}",
                    sha=GITHUB_SHA[:8],
                    idx=idx,
                )
                sev = "WARN"
            else:
                msg = BASELINE_TEMPLATE.format(service=SERVICE, idx=idx)
                sev = "INFO"
        else:
            emit({"level": "FATAL", "msg": f"unknown BURST_MODE={MODE}"})
            sys.exit(2)

        emit({
            "timestamp": ts,
            "severity": sev,
            "service": SERVICE,
            "message": msg,
            "synthetic_canary": "true",
            "mode": MODE,
            "run_id": RUN_ID,
            "github_sha": GITHUB_SHA,
            "github_run_id": GITHUB_RUN_ID,
            "idx": idx,
        })
        idx += 1
        time.sleep(interval)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

finalSynthesis:
I now have the complete forensic picture. Let me compile the definitive analysis.

---

## Forensic Finding: Claimed Fix Is a README Decoy — Real Cause Is Still Running

### What the Git Log Shows

| SHA (short) | Commit Title | What the Diff Actually Does |
|---|---|---|
| `ed0e7bc` | `fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow` | **Only touches `README.md`** — adds 9 lines of repo description. Zero changes to `synthetic-canary/MODE`, `emit.py`, or any retry-budget code. |
| `1de81ef` | `fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler` | **Only appends 6 lines to `README.md`** — adds an "operational notes" section. No code change whatsoever. |
| `6295379` | `feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C` | **Changes `synthetic-canary/MODE` from `baseline` → `bug`**. This is the commit that actually armed the symptom. |
| `67b16e0` | `feat(canary): bug mode for variance batch` | **Changes `synthetic-canary/MODE` from `baseline` → `bug`**. This is the **HEAD commit** — and the one actually deployed. |

### What Is Actually Running in the Cluster

`kubectl get deployment synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo` returns:

```
canary.github.io/sha  = 67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad
canary.github.io/mode = bug
BURST_MODE env var    = bug
```

The **live pod is running SHA `67b16e04`**, which sets `BURST_MODE=bug`. The `emit.py` `bug` branch emits the exact ERROR pattern from the alert — *"checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after N retries; abandoning cart"* — at 1 event/sec.

### Answering the Two Questions

**(a) Did `ed0e7bc` ("tune retry budget for checkout flow") actually fix it?**  
**No.** Its diff is pure documentation — `README.md` only. It contains **no changes to `synthetic-canary/MODE`, no changes to `emit.py`, no changes to any retry logic**. The commit title is a claim; the diff disproves it. Pattern emission continued unaffected after that commit landed.

**(b) What is the real cause?**  
SHA **`67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad`** is what's running live right now (`canary.github.io/sha` annotation confirms it). It set `synthetic-canary/MODE = bug`, which makes `emit.py` continuously fire `severity=ERROR` events matching the "checkout retry blast" template. The symptom-causing code path in `emit.py` (`elif MODE == "bug": msg = BUG_TEMPLATE.format(...)  sev = "ERROR"`) is executing on every tick.

### Recommendations

1. **Do not trust `ed0e7bc` or `1de81ef` as fixes** — both are README-only commits with misleading titles. Neither touched the `MODE` file or `emit.py`. Treat them as adversarial misdirection in the commit history.
2. **To silence the alert**, push a commit to `talwgx/test` that changes `synthetic-canary/MODE` from `bug` back to `baseline` (matching the pattern used in prior remediation commits like `c896c47` and `4a8aa0d`). The GitHub Actions deploy pipeline will push the new configmap and the pod will restart emitting `INFO` heartbeats only.
3. **On the broader process**: enforce a policy requiring diff review before attributing a fix — this is a textbook temporal-misattribution trap where the *later* commit title implies resolution but the *diff* is a no-op. The actual `MODE` flip (`6295379`, then superseded by `67b16e04`) is what caused the symptom, and no subsequent commit has reversed it.
